_note: STAGING — stress-test encodings for the relocation schema. Proven here against the engine, THEN merged into states.json facts and rolled to all 51. Not read by any live tool.

estateTax.note: Inheritance tax 0% spouse / 4.5% lineal / 12-15% others; no exemption floor for non-spouse.

estateTax.note: ~$7.16M exemption with a notorious cliff (estates ~5% over exemption lose the WHOLE exemption).

NY._approximation: NY tax-benefit recapture provision not modeled: bracket-sum understates by ~5% (~$280 at $100k taxable) for incomes above ~$107,650 where NY claws back the lower-bracket benefit. For relocation comparison at typical retiree incomes this is immaterial to rankings, but flag for precise-mode. Validated: single $100k computes $5,432 vs published $5,714.

taxContext.salesTaxNote: NO state or local sales tax
General Excise Tax 4-4.5% (taxes services, applied broadly); groceries taxed
6.25% no local add-on; groceries, clothing under $175 exempt
6% state; local option up to 1% (~6.39% combined); groceries, clothing exempt

VT._note: SS phaseout uses VT DOR exact thresholds (tax.vermont.gov): single full<$55k/phase 55-65k/zero>=65k; joint full<$65k/phase 70-80k/zero>=80k. Single brackets from audited states.json baseline; joint ~2x (mid thresholds approximate, refine in rollout validation).

NO general sales tax (8.5% meals & rooms tax); Interest & Dividends tax REPEALED Jan 1 2025 (never applied to IRA income)

NH._note: I&D tax repealed eff Jan 1 2025 (TY2026 fully zero on retirement income). High property tax (1.61%) is NH primary revenue — the relevant Tier-2 cost for a NH retiree.

taxContext.salesTaxNote: state 5.6%; ~8.4% combined; groceries exempt at state level
state 6.25%; ~8.85% combined; groceries 1% (being eliminated)

IL._note: Flat 4.95% on wages/cap-gains but ALL qualified retirement income (pension, IRA, 401k, conversions) is exempt -> a retiree drawing from SS+pension+IRA pays $0 IL income tax. Among the income-tax-but-retirement-exempt club (cf PA, MS). High property tax (2.08%) is the real IL cost.

taxContext.salesTaxNote: flat 7% state, no local add-on; groceries exempt

IN._note: Counties levy local income tax (~1-3%, Tier-2: not modeled). State rate 2.95% falling to ~2.9% 2027.

taxContext.salesTaxNote: flat 6% state, no local add-on; groceries exempt

estateTax.note: Inheritance tax 0% for immediate family (Class A); 4-16% for others.

taxContext.salesTaxNote: flat 6% state, no local add-on; groceries exempt

MI._note: 2026 retirement deduction phase-in complete (PA4 2023): $65,987 single/$131,794 joint covers pension+IRA+401k. A couple under the cap pays $0 on retirement income despite the 4.25% rate.

taxContext.salesTaxNote: state 4.75%; ~7% combined; groceries exempt at state level

NC._note: Rate phasing down 3.99% (2026) toward 2.99% by 2028. Some military/Bailey-settlement pensions exempt but private IRA fully taxable.

taxContext.salesTaxNote: state 4.85% (1.75% on groceries); ~7.2% combined

UT._note: SS-taxing flat state: a nonrefundable SS credit phases out above $45k single/$75k joint MAGI (modeled as SS exemption threshold). Retirement credit small/income-limited -> IRA effectively taxed at 4.5%.

taxContext.salesTaxNote: state 4%; ~9.3% combined (among highest); groceries taxed at reduced 3%

AL._note: Defined-benefit PENSIONS fully exempt but IRA/401k taxable ($6k excl 65+). Federal income tax deductible from AL income (lowers effective rate; not modeled — slight overstate).

taxContext.salesTaxNote: 6% (10% meals/alcohol); groceries, prescriptions exempt
NO sales tax

DE._note: Same brackets all filing statuses. $12,500 retirement exclusion (60+) covers IRA+pension combined.

taxContext.salesTaxNote: state 4%; ~7.4% combined; groceries exempt at state level

GA._note: $65k/person exclusion (65+; $130k joint) covers IRA/pension -> most retirees fully sheltered. Flat 4.99% (HB463 2026).

taxContext.salesTaxNote: state 6%; ~6.9% combined; groceries exempt

IA._note: Flat 3.8% but ALL retirement income exempt at 55+ (no cap) -> retiree 55+ pays $0 on IRA/pension (cf IL/MS/PA). Inheritance tax fully phased out 2025.

taxContext.salesTaxNote: state 6.5% (state grocery tax repealed 2025); ~8.7% combined

KS._note: SS fully exempt (SB1). KPERS/federal/military pensions exempt but PRIVATE IRA fully taxable (modeled retirement as taxed for portfolio retiree).

taxContext.salesTaxNote: state 4.225%; ~8.4% combined; groceries 1.225%

MO._note: FIRST STATE to fully exempt capital gains (HB594, TY2026): 100%% subtraction of federal cap gains -> a retiree living off brokerage gains pays $0 MO on them. SS exempt 62+. Private pension $6k exclusion income-limited (mostly doesnt help IRA). Same brackets all statuses. Kansas City/St Louis local earnings tax (Tier-2: not modeled).

taxContext.salesTaxNote: state 7% (groceries taxed at full 7%, among few that do); ~7.1% combined

MS._note: ALL qualified retirement income (IRA/401k/pension) exempt at 59.5+ -> retiree pays $0 on it (cf IL/IA/PA). Flat 4.4% (stepping to 3.75%). Early (<59.5) distributions taxed.

taxContext.salesTaxNote: NO general sales tax (some resort-town local taxes)

MT._note: SS-taxing on federal formula. Repealed broad pension/IRA deduction 2025 -> only ~$5,500 retirement deduction; IRA effectively taxed. Top rate 5.65% (2026), 5.4% 2027.

taxContext.salesTaxNote: state 5%; ~7% combined; groceries exempt

ND._note: Among lowest rates (top 2.5%). 0% bottom bracket shelters ~$47k single/$79k joint. ND excludes 40% of net long-term capital gains (general exclusion, not source-restricted).

taxContext.salesTaxNote: state 5.5%; ~6.97% combined; groceries exempt

estateTax.note: County inheritance tax: 1% lineal (over $100k), higher for others. No estate tax.

NE._note: SS fully exempt 2025+. IRA fully taxable, no exclusion. Top 4.55% (2026), 3.99% 2027. High property tax (1.63%).

taxContext.salesTaxNote: state 5.75%; ~7.24% combined; groceries exempt

OH._note: Flat 2.75% over $26,050 (HB96 2026); 0% below. SS exempt, military retirement exempt. Municipalities levy local income tax but generally NOT on retirement distributions (Tier-2: not modeled).

taxContext.salesTaxNote: state 5.3%; ~5.77% combined; groceries 1%

VA._note: Same brackets all statuses; top 5.75% at just $17k -> essentially flat 5.75% for retirees. $12k age-65 deduction phases out above $50k single/$75k joint AGI (a conversion can erase it; not modeled as cliff here — slight understate for high earners).

taxContext.salesTaxNote: state 6%; ~6.57% combined; groceries exempt

WV._note: SS FULLY exempt 2026 (phase-out complete). $8k retirement modification (65+) partially shelters IRA. Top rate 4.58% (SB392 2026, cutting most years). Same brackets all statuses.

_hardening.note: bracketsByStatus expanded to {single,mfj,mfs,hoh}. The 33 single==mfj states (flat/no-tax/same-threshold) have all four identical (verified by structure). The graduated states below need primary-source verification of mfj (some currently ~2x approximations), mfs, and hoh. NY mfj also needs recapture-aware correction.
